findings.description: The North Carolina state seal image at the top of the letter is not present in the HTML rendering.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 shows the NC state seal centered at the top. HTML page 1 does not display this seal/image.

findings.description: The HTML rendering collapses the letterhead layout. The PDF has officials' names (Roy Cooper, Michael S. Regan, Brian Wrenn) on the left and the seal/state name on the right in a multi-column header. The HTML renders these as a single column at the bottom of the page rather than at the top.
findings.evidence: PDF: Officials listed top-left, seal top-center, state name top-right. HTML: Officials appear below the DEQ logo block at bottom, after the letter content from the first scanned page.

findings.description: The numbered list items and sub-content on page 1 lose some indentation structure in the HTML compared to the PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF shows items 1-4 with consistent indentation under the RE: block. HTML renders them with slightly different indentation but content is preserved.

findings.description: The DEQ logo appears in the HTML rendering but is positioned differently than in the PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF: DEQ logo at bottom of page with contact info to the right. HTML: DEQ logo appears similarly but in a slightly different layout context.

findings.description: Underlined text formatting for 'Certified Mail' and 'Return Receipt Requested' appears preserved in the HTML but with slight visual differences.
findings.evidence: PDF shows underlined text clearly. HTML shows similar underlined text.

findings.description: The HTML reading order on page 2 (combined pages) places the plain text version of the content after the scanned image version, creating duplication of content rather than showing it once.
findings.evidence: The HTML appears to render both a scanned image version and a text version of the same page content sequentially, creating redundant reading.

findings.description: The sub-item formatting under item 5 (A through G) is slightly different in the HTML. The PDF uses hanging indent formatting while HTML uses a more standard list format.
findings.evidence: PDF: Items A-G have specific indentation under item 5. HTML: Items A-G rendered as a continuous paragraph in the text version.

findings.description: On the page containing Page Three content, the HTML renders the scanned version followed by a full text duplicate, creating redundant content.
findings.evidence: PDF: Single page three with basin details. HTML: Shows the scanned page three image content followed by the same content in text form.

findings.description: On page 3, the basin-by-basin listing in the HTML text version loses its structured paragraph formatting - items A-G from page 2 are rendered as a single run-on paragraph.
findings.evidence: PDF: Each sub-item (A through G) is a separate paragraph. HTML text version: Items are merged into a continuous paragraph.

findings.description: Page Four content is also duplicated - scanned version followed by text version.
findings.evidence: PDF: Single page four. HTML: Both scanned and text versions appear sequentially.

findings.description: The handwritten signature of Judith A. Wehner is absent from the HTML text rendering on page 4.
findings.evidence: PDF shows a handwritten signature above the typed name. HTML text version only shows 'Sincerely,' and typed name without the signature image.

findings.description: The bold formatting of 'two (2)' on page 3/4 is preserved in the HTML.
findings.evidence: Both PDF and HTML show 'two (2)' in bold.

findings.description: No heading hierarchy is present in the HTML. The document lacks any h1-h6 tags. The letter's subject line (RE:), numbered items, and sub-sections are not structured with headings at all.
findings.evidence:
The HTML uses only

and

    tags. No h1, h2, or h3 elements exist for the document title, RE: line, or section headers like the erosion and sediment control measures section.

findings.description: Full-page embedded images are included alongside the full text transcription of each page, resulting in content duplication. Each page's content appears both as an image and as text.
findings.evidence: For each of pages 1-4, there is an tag (e.g., alt='Scanned document page 2') followed by the full text content of that page. This means each page's content is represented twice in the HTML.

findings.description: Alt text for embedded page images is generic rather than descriptive.
findings.evidence: Images have alt text like 'Scanned document page 2', 'Scanned document page 3', 'Scanned document page 4' which are generic descriptions rather than meaningful content descriptions.

findings.description: Sub-items under item 5 (A through G) are formatted as a continuous paragraph with line-break-like spacing rather than as a proper lettered sub-list, reducing readability but not losing content.
findings.evidence: PDF shows items A-G as separate lettered sub-items. HTML presents them as a single paragraph block with 'A. ... B. ... C. ...' run together.
